destinations.evidence: Scheduled obligation windows are TTL bounded and computed by the shared helper before obligation emission.
destinations.snippet: pub const SCHEDULER_MAX_TTL_BLOCKS: u64 = 500; due_index_bounds rejects ttl > cap and returns inclusive first/last due indices; compute_obligations emits each unexecuted due obligation.

destinations.evidence: Builder augmentation uses the same due-window helper and active-job handling.
destinations.snippet: Ok(true) => SkipActiveJob; Ok(false) | Err(_) => Proceed; compute_obligations_from_calls reuses due_index_bounds

destinations.evidence: Payload builder consumes bounded scheduled obligations.
Verifier uses the same bounded obligation source for censorship checks.
destinations.snippet: let obligations = compute_obligations(...)?; for obligation in &obligations { ... }

equivalence_argument: Mapped in-port: due_index_bounds defines exact TTL-bounded windows and invalid/no-due cases, while builder and verifier consume the same bounded obligation set and active-job handling.

related_but_insufficient_code: Dependency repos did not contain the scheduler obligation owner symbols.

notes: Mapped directly in scheduled verification/block-building with active payload/verifier consumers.
rationale: The obligation-generation candidates define the current block-building side of scheduled validity: obligations remain due over TTL windows, pathological TTL/arithmetic is bounded, and active async jobs suppress augmentation only on a confirmed active result.
statement: Scheduled obligation computation must emit obligations for every block from expected_block through expected_block + ttl inclusive, skip calls whose ttl exceeds SCHEDULER_MAX_TTL_BLOCKS of 500, use saturating arithmetic for expected/deadline/expiry calculations with zero num_calls expiring at 0, and during augmentation skip synthesis only when the async active-job check returns Ok(true), proceeding on Ok(false) or storage/read errors so the consensus post-filter can decide.
suggested_falsification_tests: For start_block 100, frequency 10, ttl 3, and index 1, assert obligations appear at blocks 110 through 113 inclusive and not at 109 or 114.
Assert calls with ttl 501 are skipped by contract-state and pool-state obligation paths, and near-limit arithmetic saturates rather than wraps or panics.
Mock job_exists_and_active to return Ok(true), Ok(false), and Err; assert augmentation skips only for Ok(true) and synthesizes for Ok(false) and Err.
summary: Scheduled obligations span bounded TTL windows in the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Assert due_index_bounds includes indices within expected_block..expected_block+ttl, rejects ttl 501, zero calls/frequency, and that active-job decision skips only Ok(true).
title: Scheduled obligations span bounded TTL windows
